metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's exercised primitive is parser robustness: the transcript tailer + line parser must ingest a single JSONL line that exceeds normal size WITHOUT stalling, so the session still arcs `ready → working → ready` (no stuck `working`). No special agent capability is required.

**agent_supports: yes.** kiro-cli writes an append-only JSONL transcript under `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl`, one `\n`-terminated record per event (Prompt / AssistantMessage / ToolResults / Clear — FINDINGS.md §4). A `ToolResults` event embeds the FULL tool-output JSON in one line, so a single tool call that reads a large file produces one oversized JSONL line naturally — there is nothing kiro-specific to 'support', the flusher writes whatever the tool returned terminated by `\n`. So agent_supports is trivially yes (the same way it is for every JSONL adapter).

**daemon_capability: full.** kiro is a line_based adapter: `core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go` declares `Source: agent.FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: agent.JSONLineParser{...}}`, so kiro transcripts run through the SHARED tailer — the oversized-line handling is parser-agnostic. `core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go` reads each line via `bufio.NewReaderSize(file, 64*1024)` + a custom `readLineCapped` (NOT `bufio.Scanner`, whose old ~2 MB cap would wedge), with `maxTranscriptLineSize = 64 MB` (issue #270). A line under the cap parses normally; a line beyond it is logged ('skipping oversized line at offset N') and the offset is advanced past it so processing continues with the next line; an `errPartialAtEOF` re-read path handles a partially-written giant line. kiro's `JSONLineParser` + `kirocli.Parser` run on top of this, so kiro inherits the identical robustness as claudecode/codex/pi — the cap path is shared, agent-agnostic code, already pinned by `core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go` (#270 regression). The kiro parser then maps the post-tool text-only `AssistantMessage` to `turn_done`, driving the working→ready settle.

**driver_capability: ready.** The recipe needs only `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep`, all of which the kiro interactive driver elicits (`DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` in `replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh`). No `keys`/signal/multi-session primitive is required, so there is no driver gap.

Net for the cell: a large tool result (file read) flushes one big JSONL line; it either fits under 64 MB (parsed normally) or is logged-and-skipped past — either way the `ready → working → ready` arc completes. The completion of the working→ready transition IS the parser-resilience proof: had the tailer wedged on the long line, that transition would never fire and the phase contract would time out.

**Observations.** kiro's JSONL carries NO model / token / cost fields — those live in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar, which the v1 parser does NOT read (FINDINGS.md §4, kirocli/parser.go). So the recording's observation vector reports model `unknown` and zero cost/tokens; the spec asserts NO hard model/cost/tokens metrics (matching the pi line_based precedent), only the user-observable state arc.
caveats: A live kiro-cli session cannot be deterministically driven to emit a >64 MB (or even >2 MB) single JSONL line; the recipe provokes a LARGE single line by having kiro read a sizable file in one tool call (ToolResults embeds the full output → ~tens of KB on one line), which is well under the 64 MB cap. So this live recording is a sanity-check that the arc survives a large single line — the genuine >64 MB cap behavior is pinned by the agent-agnostic unit test core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go (readLineCapped / skip-past, issue #270), not by this recording. RECIPE NOTE (record 2026-06-05): kiro flushes its <uuid>.jsonl atomically at TURN END (probed: file stays 0 bytes the whole turn, then both Prompt+AssistantMessage land together), and the driver's wait_turn/resolve_transcript only finds a NON-EMPTY file — so a slow turn (the original 5000-word single-sentence prompt took >30s to generate) overruns the 30s transcript-resolution window and the driver bails 'never created a session file'. The recorded recipe therefore creates the big file with a fast shell tool call and reads it back in ONE turn (~12s), producing a ~60 KB single ToolResults line while staying inside the driver windows.

Lines above the 64 MB cap are dropped silently from the parser's point of view (offset advances, content lost) and surface only as an operator log line; the spec asserts the state arc, not content, so this is fine for the cell.

kiro JSONL has no model/token/cost (those live in the unread <uuid>.json sidecar), so the recording's observation vector is model=unknown, cost=0, tokens=0; the spec asserts no hard metric and relies on the state arc only.

daemon=full for the large-single-line arc is grounded in the shared-tailer code path (which kiro provably runs on via FilesUnderRoot{JSONLineParser}); the LIVE settle on a kiro oversized line is promoted from provisional to settled by record.

sources.note: Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser} — kiro transcripts run through the shared tailer, inheriting the same oversized-line handling as every JSONL adapter.
ToolResults event embeds the full tool-output JSON in one line; a text-only AssistantMessage maps to turn_done (the working→ready settle). No model/token/cost read from JSONL — sidecar-only.
bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped + maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB; oversized lines logged ('skipping oversized line at offset N') and skipped past, not stalled — references issue #270.

Agent-agnostic regression for #270: TestTailAndProcess_LargeToolResultLine (3 MB line still yields turn_done) + TestReadLineCapped_OversizedLineSkipped (direct skip path). Authoritative coverage for the cap.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" — the interactive driver elicits exactly the step types the recipe needs; no driver gap.

Live kiro-cli 2.5.1 smoke (§4,§5): JSONL events Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear, no model/usage/cost in JSONL; text-only final AssistantMessage = turn_done; ToolResults carry full tool output.
Oversized JSONL line wedge fixed by switching the shared tailer from bufio.Scanner to bufio.Reader + readLineCapped.

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; FINDINGS.md §7)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, isolated mode)
setup: No external staging: the single recipe turn has kiro create big.txt itself via a shell tool (yes x | head -c 60000 | tr -d newline > big.txt) and then read it back. The read tool result embeds the ~60 KB file content as ONE JSONL ToolResults line — the oversized single line under test. run-cell.sh does not execute recipe setup steps, so the file must be produced inside the recorded turn, not pre-staged.

script.text: Do these two steps in order: (1) run the shell command  yes x | head -c 60000 | tr -d "\n" > big.txt  to create a file, then (2) use your file reading tool to read big.txt and tell me only how many characters are on its first line.

verify: events.jsonl shows the kiro session (UUID stem of ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl) born ready within ~2s of REPL launch (a transient proc-<PID> pre-session may also appear at launch; the turn arc + pid_discovered land on the UUID-keyed session)
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition for the kiro session as the large-reply turn begins

events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end — completion of the arc is the parser-resilience proof; if the tailer wedged on the oversized single line this transition would never fire (the genuine >64 MB cap behavior is pinned by core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go, issue #270; this live recording is a sanity-check on a large single line)
events.jsonl shows a pid_discovered event bound to the kiro session
irrlichd logs do NOT contain a 'skipping oversized line' warning for the session's transcript (a large file read / 5000-word reply is well under the 64 MB cap)
no state_transition to waiting appears for the session at any point
final session state is ready before the driver tears the TUI down
transcript.jsonl contains 1 Prompt line and at least 1 text-only AssistantMessage line; the large reply / tool result is flushed as a single JSONL row with no embedded newlines
